what: Split-K path uses BLOCK_SIZE_K=512, NUM_KSPLIT=2, and BLOCK_SIZE_N=64
why: The new 2-way split-K branch likely loses too much occupancy because it launches only 68 GEMM blocks for the 16x2112x7168 shape. Halving N tile width doubles the split-K GEMM grid to 132 blocks while keeping the low 2-way reduction count, which may recover latency hiding without returning to the heavier 7-way reduction path.

what: Split-K path uses BLOCK_SIZE_K=512, NUM_KSPLIT=2, BLOCK_SIZE_N=64, and num_stages=2
why: v233 tests whether a narrower N tile can recover occupancy on the low-reduction 2-way split-K path. If the extra blocks help, restoring num_stages=2 may then recover memory/compute overlap that was removed in the v232 low-pressure experiment. This isolates whether BSN=64 works best with the original pipelined schedule.
override: This is a distinct follow-up to the BSN64 experiment: same 2-way split-K geometry but with a different pipelining point, which has not been measured on the BSN64 branch.

what: Split-K path uses BLOCK_SIZE_K=512, NUM_KSPLIT=2, BLOCK_SIZE_N=64, num_stages=2, and GROUP_SIZE_M=2
why: For the 16x2112x7168 split-K shape, BSM=8 creates exactly two M tiles. Setting GROUP_SIZE_M=2 should schedule those paired M tiles adjacently for each N chunk so they reuse the same B tile stream more closely in L2 while keeping the higher 132-block grid from the BSN64 branch.

what: Split-K path uses BLOCK_SIZE_K=512, NUM_KSPLIT=2, BLOCK_SIZE_N=64, num_stages=2, GROUP_SIZE_M=2, and num_warps=8
why: The BSN64 two-way split-K branch improved steadily from v233 to v235, which suggests occupancy was part of the problem. With smaller 64-wide N tiles and only two K splits, each block has less work than the original 7-way split path. Raising num_warps from 4 to 8 may hide memory latency better on this narrower-tile branch without reintroducing extra reduction cost.

what: Split-K path uses BLOCK_SIZE_K=512, NUM_KSPLIT=2, BLOCK_SIZE_N=64, num_stages=2, GROUP_SIZE_M=2, and waves_per_eu=1
why: On the BSN64 two-way split-K branch, occupancy improved as more launch geometry was exposed, but the explicit waves_per_eu=2 hint may now be over-constraining register allocation. Reducing the hint to 1 keeps an occupancy floor while giving the compiler more room than the current setting on the narrower-tile branch.
